 {"id":"bd-1uiu4","title":"Task: Unit tests for encryption library","description":"## Scope
Write comprehensive unit tests for the AES-GCM encryption helper.

## Cases
- Round-trip encrypt/decrypt with known key.
- Wrong key returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Corrupted header / corrupted ciphertext returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Nonce randomness: two encryptions of same plaintext differ.

## Logging
- Tests should log:
  - format version
  - ciphertext length
  - error types for negative cases

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are hermetic and do not persist secrets.
- Uses only synthetic plaintext fixtures.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:39.966085602Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:39.979530616Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","depends_on_id":"bd-29cp6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","depends_on_id":"bd-z7ses","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1unv","title":"EPIC: Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning","description":"**Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning EPIC**

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Architecture (Existing + Planned)
- **Metrics definitions:** `internal/scoring/metrics.go` (core metrics + weights)
- **Score persistence + trends:** `internal/scoring/tracker.go` (JSONL, retention, trend windows)
- **Integration:** `ntm assign` + dashboard panel + robot/analytics export

## Data Sources (Deterministic)
- Task lifecycle signals (beads: assigned → in_progress → closed)
- Retry counts (re-sent prompts / reassignments)
- Time-to-complete (timestamps in task + session logs)
- Token usage (BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator)
- Quality gates (tests/CI pass, regressions)

## Learning / Scoring Rules
- **Cold start:** fallback to neutral baseline until minimum sample threshold met
- **Stability:** use rolling window + decay (recent scores weighted higher)
- **Fairness:** per-task-type normalization to avoid skew from harder tasks
- **No gaming:** cap extreme values, validate sources, log anomalies

## Success Criteria
1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted (JSONL)
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed deterministically
4. Scores influence recommendations with explainable rationale
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings + trends
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (>=80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy + recommendation influence

## Notes
- Retention defaults to 90 days; configurable
- Scores must be reproducible for the same input set","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1uunz","title":"Implement agent launch sequence after exit","description":"Implement agent launch sequence after successful exit.

## Steps
1. Send 'cc' Enter to pane
2. Wait for claude code initialization (6 seconds typical)
3. Verify claude code is ready (check for prompt or ready indicator)

## Timing
- cc command: immediate
- Initialization wait: 6 seconds
- Ready check: poll for up to 10 seconds

## Detection
Look for:
- Claude Code prompt character
- 'Claude Code' banner text
- Ready indicator in output

## Error Handling
- If cc command fails to start: return error with shell output
- If initialization times out: return error with pane content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:12.846348928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358201896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358098031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uunz","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1v1y","title":"Task: Implement proactive compaction trigger","description":"## Purpose
Automatically trigger context compaction when prediction threshold crossed.

## Trigger Logic
if prediction.ShouldCompact && config.AutoCompact {
    // Determine agent type
    switch pane.Type {
    case AgentClaude:
        send '/compact' to pane
    case AgentCodex:
        send '!compact' to pane  // or equivalent
    case AgentGemini:
        // Gemini-specific compaction
    }
    
    // Wait for compaction to complete
    waitForCompaction(pane)
    
    // Inject recovery context
    injectRecoveryContext(pane)
}

## Scope
- Detect ShouldCompact from predictor
- Send appropriate compact command per agent type
- Wait for compaction completion
- Inject recovery context after compaction
- Notify user of automatic compaction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compaction triggered before context exhaustion
- Correct command sent per agent type
- Recovery context injected post-compaction
- User notification in dashboard/alerts

## Technical Notes
Reuse session recovery infrastructure
Integrate with notification system","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-35sy7","title":"Feature: Privacy Mode (ephemeral sessions + no persistence)","description":"## Summary
Add a privacy-focused mode for sensitive work where NTM avoids persisting potentially sensitive data (prompt history, event logs, output archives) to disk.

## Why
Even with redaction, some users prefer a stronger guarantee: **don’t store it at all**.

## Proposed UX
- Config: `[privacy] enabled = true` (or a profile-style toggle).
- CLI: `ntm spawn <session> --privacy` (session-scoped).
- Robot: include `privacy_mode` in `--robot-status/--robot-snapshot`.

## Semantics
When privacy mode is enabled for a session:
- Prompt history is disabled (or stored in-memory only).
- Event logging is disabled or stores minimal metadata (no message bodies).
- Output archives/checkpoints either:
  - are disabled, OR
  - require explicit `--allow-persist`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear indicators in dashboard/status output.
- Safe defaults (no silent persistence).
- Unit tests for config + gating.
- E2E test to ensure no files are written for a privacy session.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:52.448290337Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:52.453774467Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35sy7","depends_on_id":"bd-27hn0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35vy9","title":"E2E Tests: AutoRespawner full cycle with real tmux","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end tests for AutoRespawner using real tmux sessions to verify the complete respawn cycle works in practice.

## Test Script

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# e2e_auto_respawner.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RESPAWN]"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_respawn_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up test session: $TEST_SESSION"
    tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting AutoRespawner E2E tests"

# Test 1: Create test session and simulate stuck agent
log "Test 1: Graceful agent kill"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "sleep 3600" Enter

sleep 1
log "Agent simulated (sleep process)"

# Attempt graceful kill (Ctrl+C)
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" C-c
sleep 1

# Verify process stopped
PANE_PID=$(tmux display-message -p -t "$TEST_SESSION" "#{pane_pid}")
log "Pane PID: $PANE_PID"

if \! pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass "Graceful kill worked"
else
    fail "Graceful kill did not stop process"
fi

# Test 2: Force kill fallback
log "Test 2: Force kill fallback"
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "trap \"\" SIGINT; sleep 3600" Enter
sleep 1

# Ctrl+C should be ignored
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" C-c
sleep 1

if pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Process still running (as expected for force kill test)"
    
    # Force kill the child process
    CHILD_PID=$(pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    if [[ -n "$CHILD_PID" ]]; then
        log "Killing child PID: $CHILD_PID"
        kill -9 "$CHILD_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
        sleep 1
        
        if \! pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            pass "Force kill worked"
        else
            fail "Force kill did not stop process"
        fi
    fi
else
    pass "Unexpected: graceful kill worked on trapped process"
fi

# Test 3: Clear pane and respawn
log "Test 3: Clear pane and respawn"
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "clear" Enter
sleep 0.5
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "echo AGENT_READY" Enter
sleep 0.5

PANE_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$TEST_SESSION" -p | tail -5)
log "Pane output: $PANE_OUTPUT"

if echo "$PANE_OUTPUT" | grep -q "AGENT_READY"; then
    pass "Clear and respawn worked"
else
    fail "Clear and respawn failed"
fi

# Test 4: Prompt injection after respawn
log "Test 4: Prompt injection"
PROMPT="echo MARCHING_ORDERS_RECEIVED"
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "$PROMPT" Enter
sleep 0.5

PANE_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$TEST_SESSION" -p | tail -5)
if echo "$PANE_OUTPUT" | grep -q "MARCHING_ORDERS_RECEIVED"; then
    pass "Prompt injection worked"
else
    fail "Prompt injection failed"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E AutoRespawner Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
fi
exit 0
```

## Detailed Logging Requirements
- Log each test phase with [E2E-RESPAWN] prefix
- Log PIDs and process states
- Log tmux capture-pane output
- Log timing between operations
- Log cleanup on exit

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test graceful agent kill (Ctrl+C)
- [ ] Test force kill fallback for trapped processes
- [ ] Test pane clear and new agent spawn
- [ ] Test prompt injection after respawn
- [ ] Real tmux session (not mocked)
- [ ] Comprehensive logging with [E2E-RESPAWN] prefix
- [ ] Proper cleanup on exit
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, non-zero on failure","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:33:39.564162821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:06:28.475913617Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35vy9","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35xyt","title":"Implement tmux environment detection","description":"Implement tmux environment detection.

## Fields

- binary_path: Path to actual tmux binary
- version: tmux version string
- shell_alias_detected: bool - shell has tmux alias/plugin
- recommended_path: /usr/bin/tmux (always)

## Detection Logic

```bash
# Find actual binary
which tmux           # May return alias
/usr/bin/tmux -V     # Always works

# Detect shell alias
type tmux            # Shows if function/alias
```

## Shell Plugin Detection

Check for common plugins that wrap tmux:
- oh-my-zsh tmux plugin
- tmuxinator
- tmux-resurrect

## Warning Behavior

If shell_alias_detected=true, include warning in output:
```json
{
  "tmux": {
    "shell_alias_detected": true,
    "warning": "Use binary_path to avoid shell plugin interference"
  }
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:12.071097787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:38:37.177764435Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:38:28.755198779Z","close_reason":"Implemented tmux environment detection in internal/robot/env.go with comprehensive tests in env_test.go. Features: findTmuxBinaryPath (standard paths + fallback), getTmuxVersion, detectTmuxAlias (shell function/alias detection), detectOhMyZshTmuxPlugin, detectTmuxinator, detectTmuxResurrect. Also added supporting types: TmuxEnvInfo, EnvOutput, SessionStructureInfo, ShellEnvInfo, TimingInfo, TargetingInfo. 28 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35xyt","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-364w3","title":"Feature: Finding Provenance Tracking","description":"## Summary
Track complete provenance for each finding: origin mode, context snapshot, and transformations through dedupe and synthesis.

## Rationale
Provenance enables accountability, debugging, and trusted synthesis.

## Implementation
- Add stable FindingID generation (deterministic hash over normalized finding content + mode_id + context_hash).
- ProvenanceChain includes: source_mode, context_hash, timestamps, transform steps (dedupe/merge/synthesis), and evidence pointers.
- `ntm ensemble provenance <finding-id>` renders full chain (human + JSON).
- Dashboard shows provenance on click.

## Logging
- Log provenance attachment at each stage (discovery, dedupe, synthesis).

## Tests
- Unit: deterministic FindingID + chain building
- Integration: chain preserved through synthesis transformations
- E2E: CLI provenance output + JSON schema

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every finding has a complete provenance chain
- [ ] Provenance survives dedupe + synthesis transformations
- [ ] CLI command renders chain
- [ ] Dashboard visualizes provenance
- [ ] Export includes provenance metadata

## Dependencies
- OutputSchema (bd-1wnxl)
- Synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:08:36.643962845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:12:18.714960659Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:12:18.714516102Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-364w3","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-364w3","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-364w3","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":70,"issue_id":"bd-364w3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Distinction from Synthesis Explanation (bd-1a3hu)
Provenance answers WHERE: origin chain of each finding (mode→dedup→synthesis).
Explanation answers WHY: reasoning for each conclusion in synthesis output.
Provenance provides the data that Explanation references.","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:46:18Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-36dd7","title":"Task: Add child_pid field to pane status","description":"## What

Add `child_pid` field to each pane object in --robot-status output.

## Why

When soft restart (Ctrl-C) fails, operators need to kill -9 the actual agent
process. Currently this requires running `pgrep -P <shell_pid>` manually.
The child_pid field provides this directly.

## Implementation

```go
// In pane status gathering:
func getChildPID(shellPID int) (int, error) {
    // Option 1: pgrep
    out, err := exec.Command("pgrep", "-P", strconv.Itoa(shellPID)).Output()
    
    // Option 2: /proc filesystem (more portable)
    // Read /proc/<shellPID>/task/<shellPID>/children
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "pid": 12345,        // Shell PID (existing)
    "child_pid": 12346   // NEW: Agent process PID
  }]
}
```

## Edge Cases

- Multiple children: Return first (most likely the agent)
- No children: Return null (agent may have exited)
- Permission denied: Return null with error flag

## Testing

- Unit test: Mock /proc reads
- Integration test: Verify against actual tmux session","notes":"Feature already implemented in status_enrich.go. getChildPID function is called by enrichAgentStatus and populates agent.ChildPID field correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:02.623007474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:38:57.959894323Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:38:57.959807720Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36dd7","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3mr38","title":"Task: Implement ntm --robot-giil wrapper","description":"## Overview
Implement `ntm --robot-giil-fetch=URL` command that wraps `giil` to download images from share URLs.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-giil-fetch=URL
```

Example:
```bash
ntm --robot-giil-fetch="https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123"
```

## Implementation Details

1. **Flag Registration** (root.go):
```go
rootCmd.Flags().StringVar(&robotGiilFetch, "robot-giil-fetch", "", 
    "Download image from share URL via giil. Required: URL")
```

2. **Handler Logic**:
   - Check giil availability via ToolRegistry
   - Execute: `giil <url>`
   - Parse output for downloaded file path
   - Stat the file for size
   - Return structured JSON

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "path": "/path/to/downloaded/image.jpg",
  "filename": "image.jpg", 
  "size_bytes": 1234567,
  "source_url": "https://share.icloud.com/..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag registered in root.go
- [ ] Handler in robot_giil.go
- [ ] Missing giil returns actionable error
- [ ] Works with iCloud share URLs
- [ ] JSON output includes all required fields","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.

 {"id":"bd-3mwd9","title":"Task: Auth modes + safe default bind","description":"## Scope\nImplement authentication modes and safe default binding for REST/WS servers, including origin/CORS checks.\n\n## Implementation\n- Default bind: 127.0.0.1 only.\n- Require explicit flags for 0.0.0.0 + auth.\n- Auth modes: Local (none), API key, OIDC, mTLS.\n- Shared auth middleware for REST + WS.\n- CORS/origin allowlist configurable (default localhost).\n\n## Logging\n- Log auth mode at startup; log failed auth attempts with request_id.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: auth middleware allows/denies correctly.\n- Integration: ws connection rejected without token.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- External bind without auth is blocked.\n- API key and mTLS modes enforced across REST + WS.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:54:20.710503083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T21:34:38.530435920Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T21:34:38.530419219Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3mwns","title":"E2E Tests: Full swarm lifecycle scan-to-shutdown","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end test for the complete swarm lifecycle: scan projects, calculate allocations, create sessions, spawn agents, and shutdown.

## Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_swarm_lifecycle.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-SWARM]"
TEST_DIR="/tmp/ntm_e2e_swarm_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up..."
    # Kill any test sessions
    tmux list-sessions 2>/dev/null | grep "ntm_e2e_" | cut -d: -f1 | xargs -r -I{} tmux kill-session -t {}
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting Swarm Lifecycle E2E tests"
log "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"

# Setup: Create test projects with beads
log "Setup: Creating test projects"
mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/proj1/.beads" "$TEST_DIR/proj2/.beads"

# Create issues.jsonl for proj1 (10 open beads = Tier 1)
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    echo '{"id":"p1-'$i'","title":"Task '$i'","status":"open"}' >> "$TEST_DIR/proj1/.beads/issues.jsonl"
done
log "Created proj1 with 10 open beads"

# Create issues.jsonl for proj2 (3 open beads = Tier 3)
for i in $(seq 1 3); do
    echo '{"id":"p2-'$i'","title":"Task '$i'","status":"open"}' >> "$TEST_DIR/proj2/.beads/issues.jsonl"
done
log "Created proj2 with 3 open beads"

# Test 1: Scan projects
log "Test 1: Scan projects for bead counts"
SCAN_OUTPUT=$(ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --json 2>&1 || echo "{}")
log "Scan output: $SCAN_OUTPUT"

if echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | grep -q "proj1"; then
    pass "Project scan found proj1"
else
    fail "Project scan missing proj1"
fi

# Test 2: Verify allocation calculation
log "Test 2: Allocation calculation"
ALLOCATIONS=$(echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.allocations // []' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
log "Allocations: $ALLOCATIONS"

PROJ1_AGENTS=$(echo "$ALLOCATIONS" | jq -r '.[] | select(.project.name=="proj1") | .total_agents' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "proj1 total agents: $PROJ1_AGENTS"

if [[ "$PROJ1_AGENTS" -gt "0" ]]; then
    pass "Allocation calculated for proj1"
else
    fail "No allocation for proj1"
fi

# Test 3: Create sessions (without launching real agents)
log "Test 3: Session creation (dry-run)"
SESSION_COUNT=$(echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.sessions | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "Session count: $SESSION_COUNT"

if [[ "$SESSION_COUNT" -gt "0" ]]; then
    pass "Sessions planned"
else
    fail "No sessions planned"
fi

# Test 4: Verify session naming
log "Test 4: Session naming convention"
SESSIONS=$(echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.sessions[].name' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
log "Session names: $SESSIONS"

for session in $SESSIONS; do
    if [[ "$session" =~ ^(cc|cod|gmi)_agents_[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        log "  Valid session name: $session"
    else
        fail "Invalid session name: $session"
    fi
done
pass "Session naming convention"

# Test 5: Create a real test session (minimal)
log "Test 5: Real session creation"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_lifecycle_$$"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
sleep 0.5

if tmux has-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    log "Session $TEST_SESSION created"
    pass "Real session creation"
else
    fail "Failed to create real session"
fi

# Test 6: Pane creation
log "Test 6: Pane creation"
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -v
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -h
sleep 0.5

PANE_COUNT=$(tmux list-panes -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
log "Pane count: $PANE_COUNT"

if [[ "$PANE_COUNT" -ge "3" ]]; then
    pass "Multiple panes created"
else
    fail "Pane creation failed (expected 3, got $PANE_COUNT)"
fi

# Test 7: Graceful shutdown
log "Test 7: Graceful shutdown"
tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true

if ! tmux has-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "Graceful shutdown"
else
    fail "Session not killed"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E Swarm Lifecycle Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

[[ $FAILED -eq 0 ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
```

## Detailed Logging Requirements
- Log each phase with [E2E-SWARM] prefix
- Log project setup details
- Log allocation JSON output
- Log session names and counts
- Log pane counts
- Log cleanup operations

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test allocation calculation
- [ ] Test session planning
- [ ] Test session naming convention
- [ ] Test real tmux session creation
- [ ] Test pane creation
- [ ] Test graceful shutdown
- [ ] Proper cleanup on exit
- [ ] Detailed logging with [E2E-SWARM] prefix
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:35:25.159205686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:10:22.188067696Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mwns","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nde","title":"Add ntm spawn --assign convenience flag","description":"## Overview
Provide the only convenience entry for assignment: `ntm spawn --assign`. Spawns agents and immediately assigns work in one command.

## Usage
```bash
# Spawn and assign with default strategy
ntm spawn myproject --cc=4 --assign

# With specific strategy
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=2 --assign --strategy=dependency

# With init prompt first
ntm spawn myproject --cc=4 --assign --init-prompt='Read AGENTS.md first'

# With assignment limit
ntm spawn myproject --cc=4 --assign --limit=8
```

## Workflow
1. **Spawn Phase**: Create agents via normal spawn logic
2. **Ready Wait**: Wait for all agents to show ready prompt (configurable timeout)
3. **Init Phase** (optional): Send init prompt (marching orders) to all agents
4. **Assign Phase**: Invoke `ntm assign --auto` with selected strategy
5. **Report**: Return combined spawn + assign result

## Combined Output Format
```json
{
  "spawn": {
    "session": "myproject",
    "agents": [
      {"pane": 2, "type": "claude", "status": "ready"},
      {"pane": 3, "type": "claude", "status": "ready"}
    ],
    "total": 4
  },
  "init": {
    "prompt_sent": true,
    "agents_reached": 4
  },
  "assign": {
    "strategy": "balanced",
    "assigned": [...],
    "skipped": [...],
    "summary": {...}
  }
}
```

## Ready Detection
- Watch for agent CLI prompt (e.g., "claude>" or "codex>")
- Timeout after 60s (configurable via --ready-timeout)
- On timeout: Warn and continue with ready agents

## Error Handling
- Spawn failure: Return spawn error, don't attempt assign
- Init prompt failure: Log warning, continue to assign
- Assign failure: Return partial result with assign error
- Partial success: Report which agents succeeded/failed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --assign flag works on spawn
- [ ] Waits for agent ready state
- [ ] Sends init prompt if specified
- [ ] Uses ntm assign internally (no duplicate logic)
- [ ] Strategy flag plumbed through
- [ ] --limit flag plumbed through
- [ ] Combined JSON output includes all phases
- [ ] Graceful handling of partial failures
- [ ] Clear logging of each phase

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Flag parsing and validation
- Unit test: Ready detection logic
- Unit test: Error handling for each phase
- E2E test: Full spawn + assign workflow
- E2E test: Partial failure scenarios","notes":"Keep as the single convenience entry: spawn agents + assign via ntm assign strategy. Avoid adding separate swarm/distribute entry points.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:35:43.870375746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:37.388916144Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:37.388808391Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nde","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nde","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
